title: China-to-Botswana Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC — IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 for PV Inverters — Botswana BOBS / BERA / BPC Requirements
rows.target_requirement: Botswana Standards (BOS) are published by BOBS (Botswana Bureau of Standards) and predominantly adopt IEC standards. For solar PV inverters, the relevant EMC standards are IEC 62920 (Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment) and IEC 61000-3-2 (Electromagnetic compatibility — Limits for harmonic current emissions for equipment with input current up to and including 16 A per phase). These IEC standards are consistent with Botswana's IEC-aligned regulatory framework. BPC grid-connection conditions and BERA technical licensing conditions for PV generation are expected to reference IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 as the applicable EMC standards, consistent with the IEC adoption policy under BOBS. As of 2026-06-14, no publicly available standalone Botswana national technical regulation specifically imposing IEC 62920 or IEC 61000-3-2 on solar inverters has been identified in the open literature — exporters must confirm the current EMC evidence requirements directly with BOBS (bobstandards.bw), BERA (bera.co.bw), and BPC (bpc.bw) before submitting equipment documentation.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment (IEC standard referenced by BOBS BOS framework)
IEC 61000-3-2 — Electromagnetic compatibility — Limits for harmonic current emissions (equipment with input current up to 16 A per phase) (IEC standard referenced by BOBS BOS framework)
Standards Act (Cap. 74:01) — BOBS mandate to adopt IEC standards as Botswana Standards (BOS)
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC is assessed under GB/T 37408-2019 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters), which includes EMC test requirements aligned with China's domestic framework. GB/T 37408 EMC testing is conducted at CNAS-accredited Chinese laboratories. While IEC 62920 draws on IEC 61000-series test methods that are also referenced in Chinese domestic standards, the GB/T 37408 EMC test scope and referenced limits are not harmonised with IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 as applicable under the BOBS IEC-adoption framework. Chinese EMC certificates based solely on GB/T 37408 are not accepted as IEC 62920 or IEC 61000-3-2 compliance evidence for Botswana BOBS, BERA, or BPC requirements.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — includes EMC test requirements
rows.gap: Chinese GB/T 37408 EMC testing does not satisfy the IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 requirements applicable under the Botswana BOBS IEC-adoption framework and expected by BERA and BPC for grid-tied PV inverters. Exporters should obtain: (a) IEC 62920 EMC type-test report from an ILAC-accredited laboratory covering the specific inverter model; (b) IEC 61000-3-2 harmonic current emission test report for the model. These reports should be specific to the power rating and configuration of the inverter being supplied to Botswana. Where harmonic test results were obtained under Chinese grid conditions (220/380 V, 50 Hz), consider whether retesting under Botswana grid conditions (230/400 V, 50 Hz) is required. Confirm the precise EMC evidence requirements with BOBS (bobstandards.bw), BERA (bera.co.bw), and BPC (bpc.bw) before finalising the equipment compliance package.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory under the BOBS IEC-adoption framework where BOBS has declared the product category within its mandatory conformity assessment scope; expected by BERA and BPC as part of grid-connection technical compliance — confirm precise scope with BOBS, BERA, and BPC
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary — GB/T 37408 EMC testing is a requirement for voluntary CQC/CGC certification; not a standalone mandatory market-access requirement for domestic China PV inverters

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chinese GB/T 37408 EMC certificates do not satisfy IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 requirements under the Botswana BOBS IEC-adoption framework. Obtain IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 type-test reports from an ILAC-accredited laboratory for the specific inverter model. Confirm whether Botswana grid voltage conditions (230/400 V) require retesting compared to test results obtained under 220/380 V Chinese grid conditions. Verify current BOBS mandatory conformity scope, BERA technical conditions, and BPC EMC evidence requirements directly with each authority before submitting equipment documentation.

rows.requirement: BPC Grid-Connection Standards — Anti-Islanding (IEC 62116), IEC 61727, and 50 Hz / 230 V / 400 V Settings
rows.target_requirement: BPC (Botswana Power Corporation) is the national transmission and distribution utility and the grid-connection authority for solar PV systems in Botswana. BPC's network access and connection conditions for distributed and utility-scale PV require inverters to comply with IEC-referenced grid interconnection standards applicable to Botswana's 50 Hz, 230 V single-phase / 400 V three-phase grid (consistent with IEC 60038 standard voltages). Key grid-connection requirements include: (1) IEC 62116 anti-islanding test — inverters must detect loss of mains and disconnect within the standard-specified time; (2) IEC 61727 (Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface) compliance for utility interface requirements; (3) protection relay settings and reconnection delays validated for Botswana's 230/400 V, 50 Hz grid. BERA (Botswana Energy Regulatory Authority) additionally requires a generation licence or small-scale embedded generation exemption before any grid-tied PV system can operate. Inverter firmware and protection settings must be validated against Botswana BPC grid parameters — not the 220/380 V settings used in China under GB/T 156. Note: Botswana does not have a BPC-published public technical specification document equivalent to Kahramaa EP-EPP-P7-S1 in the open literature as of 2026-06-14; exporters must confirm current BPC connection requirements directly with BPC and BERA.
target_standards: IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
IEC 60038 — IEC standard voltages (underpins Botswana 230/400 V grid nominal voltages)
BERA Act (Cap. 74:04) — Botswana Energy Regulatory Authority Act governing generation licences and technical conditions for grid-tied PV
BPC network access and connection conditions — to be confirmed directly with BPC (bpc.bw)
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System, applicable to utility-scale PV via 10 kV and above), NB/T 32004 (inverter technical specifications), and domestic anti-islanding test results under Chinese grid conditions. These documents are not accepted as BPC Botswana grid-connection evidence. The key parameter difference is voltage: China's grid standard is 220/380 V at 50 Hz under GB/T 156, whereas Botswana BPC operates at 230/400 V at 50 Hz (aligned with IEC 60038). Inverter firmware protection thresholds validated for Chinese grid conditions must be re-parameterised and retested for Botswana BPC grid conditions. Additionally, China's domestic anti-islanding test results may not be performed against IEC 62116 — IEC 62116 test evidence from an ILAC-accredited laboratory is required.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese GB/T grid-connection documents do not satisfy BPC Botswana network access requirements. Exporters must prepare: (a) IEC 62116 anti-islanding test report from an ILAC-accredited laboratory; (b) IEC 61727 utility interface compliance evidence; (c) firmware protection thresholds and reconnection delays validated for Botswana's 230/400 V, 50 Hz grid — not the 220/380 V Chinese grid parameters. Where inverter firmware settings differ from factory defaults for China, re-parameterisation must be documented and, if the change is material, re-tested under the applicable IEC standard. BERA generation licence or small-scale exemption must also be obtained before grid-tied operation; the project developer typically holds this licence, but equipment must satisfy BERA's technical conditions. Confirm all current BPC connection requirements and BERA technical conditions directly with BPC (bpc.bw) and BERA (bera.co.bw) before finalising the equipment compliance package.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid connection under BPC network access conditions; BERA licensing is a separate mandatory gate for the generating entity. Specific technical requirements should be confirmed directly with BPC and BERA.
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence satisfies China's grid operator requirements but does not transfer to BPC Botswana grid-connection acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] BPC grid-connection conditions require IEC 62116 anti-islanding and IEC 61727 utility interface evidence — not covered by Chinese GB/T grid certificates. Re-parameterise inverter firmware for Botswana's 230/400 V at 50 Hz grid before testing and document the configuration. Obtain BERA generation licence or small-scale embedded generation exemption through the project developer. Verify current BPC connection requirements and BERA technical conditions directly with BPC (bpc.bw) and BERA (bera.co.bw) before finalising the equipment compliance package.

rows.requirement: BERA Licensing and BOBS Conformity — Grid-Tied Solar PV Generation Requirements
rows.target_requirement: BERA (Botswana Energy Regulatory Authority), established under the Botswana Energy Regulatory Authority Act (Cap. 74:04), is the statutory regulator for electricity generation, transmission, distribution, and supply in Botswana. Any grid-tied solar PV generation facility requires either a BERA generation licence or a qualifying exemption (e.g. small-scale embedded generation below a threshold capacity). BERA sets the technical conditions that generating equipment — including inverters — must meet to connect to the BPC grid. BOBS (Botswana Bureau of Standards), established under the Standards Act (Cap. 74:01), publishes Botswana Standards (BOS) which largely adopt IEC standards. Where BOBS has declared a product category subject to mandatory conformity assessment, suppliers must demonstrate compliance. As of 2026-06-14, the precise scope of mandatory BOBS conformity assessment for solar PV inverters should be confirmed directly with BOBS (bobstandards.bw). Botswana has excellent solar irradiation (the Kalahari and Ghanzi regions average over 6 kWh/m²/day) and is expanding utility-scale and distributed PV (including projects at Jwaneng and Mmadinare) alongside a net-billing scheme for distributed solar. This creates growing demand for imported solar inverters that can satisfy both BERA and BPC technical requirements.
target_standards: Botswana Energy Regulatory Authority Act (Cap. 74:04) — establishes BERA and its licensing powers
Standards Act (Cap. 74:01) — establishes BOBS and Botswana Standards (BOS) framework
IEC 62109-1/-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems (adopted or referenced as BOS / IEC)
IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
rows.china_equivalent: China does not have a direct equivalent to BERA licensing. Distributed and utility-scale solar PV in China is governed by National Energy Administration (NEA) policies and grid operator (State Grid, Southern Power Grid) connection rules, with equipment assessed against domestic standards (NB/T 32004, GB/T 37408, GB/T 19964-2024). Chinese product certification bodies (CQC, CGC) issue voluntary safety certificates against GB/T 37408. Neither BERA licensing nor BOBS conformity assessment is satisfied by Chinese domestic NEA approvals or CQC/CGC certificates. These must be obtained separately for the Botswana market.
china_standards: NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
rows.gap: Gap: BERA licensing (or qualifying exemption) is a mandatory gate for any grid-tied solar PV generation facility in Botswana, separate from equipment compliance. BOBS conformity assessment may apply to solar PV inverters depending on whether the product category falls within the mandatory scheme — confirm with BOBS (bobstandards.bw). Chinese domestic NEA approvals and CQC/CGC product certificates do not satisfy BERA licensing conditions or BOBS mandatory conformity requirements. Equipment must demonstrate compliance with applicable IEC standards (IEC 62109-1/-2 safety, IEC 62116 anti-islanding, IEC 61727 utility interface, IEC 62920 EMC) as referenced by BOBS and BERA. Confirm current BERA licensing thresholds, BOBS mandatory product scope, and any applicable fees and lead times directly with the relevant authorities before committing product to Botswana projects.

mandatory_status.target: BERA generation licence or exemption: Mandatory for the generating entity before any grid-tied operation. BOBS conformity assessment: Mandatory for product categories within the declared mandatory scheme — scope to be confirmed with BOBS.
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese NEA approvals and CQC/CGC product certificates are issued under China domestic regulatory frameworks — not transferable to Botswana BERA or BOBS requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] BERA licensing is a mandatory gate for any grid-tied PV generation facility in Botswana — separate from and in addition to equipment IEC compliance. BOBS conformity assessment scope for solar PV inverters must be confirmed directly with BOBS (bobstandards.bw). Chinese domestic approvals do not transfer to Botswana. Verify current BERA licensing thresholds, fees, and technical conditions at bera.co.bw, and BOBS mandatory product scope at bobstandards.bw, before committing product to any Botswana PV project.

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety Evidence — Botswana BOBS / BERA / BPC Solar PV Grid-Connection Requirements
rows.target_requirement: BOBS (Botswana Bureau of Standards) publishes Botswana Standards (BOS) that predominantly adopt IEC standards. IEC 62109-2 (Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters) and its parent standard IEC 62109-1 (Part 1: General requirements) are the applicable international safety standards for grid-connected solar PV inverters under the BOBS IEC-adoption framework. BERA (Botswana Energy Regulatory Authority) technical licensing conditions for grid-tied PV generation, and BPC (Botswana Power Corporation) network connection conditions, are expected to reference or require IEC 62109-1/-2 safety compliance for inverter equipment. Third-party type-test evidence from an ILAC-accredited laboratory is the internationally expected form of IEC 62109-1/-2 safety compliance demonstration. Note: As of 2026-06-14, no publicly available standalone Botswana national technical regulation expressly mandating IEC 62109-1/-2 for solar PV inverters has been identified in the open literature. Exporters must confirm the current safety standard requirements directly with BOBS (bobstandards.bw), BERA (bera.co.bw), and BPC (bpc.bw) before equipment submission.
target_standards: IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters (IEC standard adopted or referenced under BOBS BOS framework)
IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements (parent standard, implicitly applicable)
Standards Act (Cap. 74:01) — BOBS mandate to adopt IEC standards as Botswana Standards (BOS)
BERA Act (Cap. 74:04) — BERA technical licensing conditions for grid-tied PV generation
rows.china_equivalent: China's primary voluntary standard for PV grid-connected inverter safety is GB/T 37408-2019 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters), which covers classification, environmental conditions, safety, electrical performance, EMC, and markings. GB/T 37408 is a China domestic standard assessed by CNAS-accredited Chinese laboratories. It shares conceptual safety intent with IEC 62109-1/-2 but is not harmonised with IEC 62109 and is not accepted by BOBS, BERA, or BPC as equivalent to IEC 62109 type-test evidence for Botswana market access. Chinese CQC/CGC voluntary safety certificates based on GB/T 37408 do not satisfy Botswana IEC 62109-1/-2 safety requirements.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Chinese GB/T 37408 safety certification does not satisfy the IEC 62109-2 safety type-test requirement expected under the Botswana BOBS IEC-adoption framework and by BERA and BPC for grid-connected solar PV inverters. Exporters must obtain IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test certificates from an ILAC-accredited laboratory covering the specific inverter model or family being supplied to Botswana projects. Existing Chinese safety certificates based solely on GB/T 37408 must be supplemented with IEC 62109-1/-2 test reports before submitting equipment documentation for BOBS conformity assessment, BERA technical licence review, or BPC grid-connection approval. Botswana's climate (high ambient temperatures and dust in the Kalahari region) means that the environmental stress profile for inverters deployed in Botswana may differ from standard IEC test conditions — confirm any special environmental test requirements with the project owner and relevant authorities.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory under BOBS IEC-adoption framework where the product category falls within declared mandatory conformity assessment scope; expected by BERA as part of technical licensing conditions and by BPC as part of grid-connection approval — confirm precise requirements with BOBS (bobstandards.bw), BERA (bera.co.bw), and BPC (bpc.bw)
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary — GB/T 37408-2019 is a recommended national standard (GB/T designation); CQC/CGC voluntary certification available but not required under Chinese law

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A solar PV inverter holding only Chinese GB/T 37408 certification cannot satisfy the IEC 62109-1/-2 safety requirements expected under the Botswana BOBS IEC-adoption framework and by BERA and BPC. Plan for IEC 62109-1/-2 type testing by an ILAC-accredited laboratory before submitting equipment documentation for Botswana project review. Confirm current BOBS mandatory conformity scope, BERA technical licensing conditions, and BPC connection requirements directly with each authority at bobstandards.bw, bera.co.bw, and bpc.bw before committing to a test programme. Consider Botswana's high-temperature and dusty Kalahari environment when selecting inverter models and test configurations.

source.publisher: IEC (International Electrotechnical Commission) — standards adopted by BOBS as Botswana Standards (BOS)
